title: Field Clinical Specialist Graduate Interventional Cardiology- Milan (Floating)
Quality Engineer
description:
**Lincotek,** headquartered in Rubbiano, Parma, Italy, is a global contract manufacturer for services in markets including Industrial Gas Turbines, Aerospace and Medical Device applications, as well as a leading manufacturer of industrial coating equipment and one of the most respected producers in the Additive Manufacturing field. The Group has more than 1,800 employees located in 18 production facilities across Europe, North America and Asia.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

**ABOUT THE JOB**


Reporting to the Product Quality Manager, this role contributes to quality control throughout the production process, supporting the analysis of non\-conformities, issuing and managing supplier deviation requests to customers, handling customer complaints, and implementing corrective and preventive actions, with a focus on technical documentation, acceptability criteria, and continuous improvement.

**MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES:**

* Demonstrates thorough knowledge of company procedures and work instructions, including established best practices, and ensures their correct application.
* Understands and applies customer specifications related to products and processes, as well as requirements for the notification and management of non\-conforming products.
* Issues and manages supplier deviation requests in compliance with specific requirements, ensuring proper internal recording and traceability (ERP updates and data server archiving).
* Coordinates the investigation of internal and external non\-conformities, promoting the implementation of effective corrective and preventive actions (e.g. RCCA, 8D).
* Participates in regular customer meetings, gathering feedback and identifying opportunities for quality performance improvement.
* Manages customer source inspections, ensuring adequate quality support.
* Maintains continuous alignment on the progress of assigned production orders by actively participating in production meetings.
* Contributes to the analysis of quality performance (e.g. scrap, rework, customer complaints), providing data and reports to support the Product Quality Manager.
* Supports the verification of the correct application of acceptability criteria and quality procedures throughout the entire production process.
* Manages quality support activities related to the monitoring and resolution of production orders with open issues.
* Manages and keeps quality documentation up to date, including work instructions.
* Reviews and updates procedures within the area of responsibility, ensuring alignment with internal requirements and customer specifications.

**SUPERVISORY RESPONSIBILITY:**

Not applicable.

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE:**

* Technical or scientific high school diploma, or a bachelor’s degree in any technical or/and engineering fields.
* At least 2 years of experience in product or process quality within industrial environments.

**SKILLS:**

**Required**

* Ability to analyze technical and process\-related issues.
* Knowledge of quality tools (e.g., RCCA, 8D).
* Familiarity with ERP/MES and Quality Management Systems (QMS) for managing non\-conformities, traceability, and production data.
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).
* Good command of English, both written and spoken.
* Strong customer focus and continuous improvement mindset.
* Good communication skills, attention to detail, and a problem\-solving attitude.
* Hands\-on approach and ability to work closely with production teams on the shop floor.

**Nice to Have**

* Experience in data analysis and statistical tools (e.g., capability analysis, trend analysis, Pareto)
* Familiarity with tools such as Minitab, Excel advanced, or Power BI
* Basic knowledge of special processes (e.g., thermal spray coating, heat treatments, welding)
* Ability to read and interpret technical drawings and specifications, including acceptability criteria
* Experience in customer interface and handling quality topics (complaints, inspections, deviation requests)

**WORK ENVIRONMENT:**


This job operates in both an office environment and a manufacturing environment. The role routinely uses standard office equipment, such as computes, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines. The individual will be required to adhere to safety policies and procedures on the manufacturing floor.

**PHYSICAL DEMAND:**


While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to stand, walk, use hands to finger, handle or feel and reach with hands and arms.


The employee is occasionally required to sit, climb or balance and stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl. The employee must frequently lift and/or move up to 5 kg and occasionally lift and or move up to 20 kg. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**WORK ENVIRONMENT:**
---------------------

  

This job operates in both an office environment and a manufacturing environment. The role routinely uses standard office equipment, such as computers, phones, photocopiers, filing cabinets and fax machines. The individual will be required to adhere to safety policies and procedures on the manufacturing floor.

 **PHYSICAL DEMAND:**

  

Limited physical demand may be needed. Physical demands will involve standing, walking, and occasionally moving or lifting material.

 **POSITION TYPE/EXPECTED HOURS OF WORK:**

  

This is a full\-time position, with business core hours of Monday through Friday 8am to 5 pm. As an exempt employee, you will be required to work additional hours to support business demands.

**TRAVEL**
----------


Occasional overseas and domestic travel may be required to support the rapid growth of the company.

  

Lincotek provides equal employment opportunity to all individuals regardless of their race, color, creed, religion, gender, age, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, federal, or local law.

title: CRA (Level II)
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


Join Us as a Clinical Research Associate (Level II) – Make an Impact at the Forefront of Innovation  

We have successfully supported the top 50 pharmaceutical companies and more than 750 biotechs, spanning 2,700 clinical trials across 100\+ countries in the last 5 years.  

  

As part of our global team, you’ll have the opportunity to perform and coordinate all aspects of the clinical monitoring and site management process. As a Clinical Research Associate (Level II), you'll conduct remote or on\-site visits to assess protocol and regulatory compliance and manage required documentation. You will manage procedures and guidelines from different sponsors and/or monitoring environments. Acting as a site processes specialist, you will ensure that the trial is conducted in accordance with the approved protocol, ICH\-GCP guidelines, applicable regulations, and SOPs to guarantee subjects' rights, well\-being, and data reliability. You will ensure audit readiness and develop collaborative relationships with investigational sites. Detailed tasks and responsibilities assigned to your role are outlined in the task matrix.  

  

What You’ll Do:  

* Monitors investigator sites with a risk\-based monitoring approach applies root cause analysis (RCA), critical thinking and problem\-solving skills to identify site processes failure and corrective/preventive actions to bring the site into compliance and decrease risks. Ensures data accuracy through SDR, SDV and CRF review as applicable through on\-site and remote monitoring activities. Assess investigational product through physical inventory and records review. Documents observations in reports and letters in a timely manner using approved business writing standards. Escalates observed deficiencies and issues to clinical management expeditiously and follow all issues through to resolution. May need to maintain regular contact between monitoring visits with investigative sites to confirm that the protocol is being followed, that previously identified issues are being resolved and that the data is being recorded in a timely manner. Conducts monitoring tasks in accordance with the approved monitoring plan. Participates in the investigator payment process. Ensures a shared responsibility with other project team members on issues/findings resolution. Investigates and follows\-up on findings as applicable.
* Participates in investigator meetings as necessary. Identifies potential investigators in collaboration with the client company to ensure the acceptability of qualified investigative sites. Initiates clinical trial sites according to the relevant procedures to ensure compliance with the protocol and regulatory and ICH GCP obligations, making recommendations where warranted. Performs trial close out and retrieval of trial materials.
* Ensures that required essential documents are complete and in place, according to ICH\-GCP and applicable regulations. Conducts on\-site file reviews as per project specifications.
* Provides trial status tracking and progress update reports to the Clinical Team Manager (CTM) as required. Ensures study systems are updated per agreed study conventions (e.g. Clinical Trial Management System).
* Facilitates effective communication between investigative sites, the client company and the PPD project team through written, oral and/or electronic contacts.
* Responds to company, client and applicable regulatory requirements/audits/inspections.
* Maintains \& completes administrative tasks such as expense reports and timesheets in a timely manner.
* Contributes to the project team by assisting in preparation of project publications/tools and sharing ideas/suggestions with team members.
* Contributes to other project work and initiatives for process improvement, as required.

  

Education and Experience Requirements:  

* Master degree in a life science related field or equivalent and relevant formal academic / vocational qualification.
* Previous experience that provides the knowledge, skills, and abilities to perform the job (comparable to 1 years as a clinical research monitor).
* Valid driver's license where applicable.

  

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:  

* Proven clinical monitoring skills
* Demonstrated understanding of medical/therapeutic area knowledge and medical terminology
* Demonstrated ability to attain and maintain a working knowledge of ICH GCPs, applicable regulations and procedural documents
* Well\-developed critical thinking skills, including but not limited to critical mindset, in\-depth investigation for appropriate root cause analysis and problem solving
* Ability to manage Risk Based Monitoring concepts and processes
* Good oral and written communication skills, with the ability to communicate effectively with medical personnel
* Ability to maintain customer focus through the utilization of good listening skills, attention to detail and the ability to perceive customers’ underlying issues
* Good organizational and time management skills
* Effective interpersonal skills
* Attention to detail
* Ability to remain flexible and adaptable in a wide range of scenarios
* Ability to work in a team or independently as required
* Good computer skills: solid knowledge of Microsoft Office and the ability to learn appropriate software
* Good English language and grammar skills
* Good presentation skills

  

Working Conditions and Environment:  

* Work is performed in an office/clinical/and/or home office environment with exposure to electrical office equipment.
* Frequent drives to site locations. May also include some overnight stays.
company_description: At Thermo Fisher Scientific, our professionals develop critical solutions—and build rewarding careers.

title: Principal Mechanical Design Engineer (with CAD Expertise)
description:
**Job Description:**


POSITION SUMMARY


Develop and optimize mechanical design of the disposables components and systems for TransMedics' Organ Care System (OCS™) using advanced CAD modeling. Design products and solutions, and verify them through appropriate simulation tools and testing, defining and executing the appropriate test protocols. Design components of TransMedics' Organ Care System (OCS™) while taking care of all the aspects related to the mechanical design and the manufacturability of the system and subsystems.


This specialized role focuses on ensuring optimal product design and manufacturability through engineering analysis.


RESPONSIBILITIES


This position is responsible for, but not limited to, the following:

* Responsible for product design, transforming ideas into concept design and defining technical solutions of Transmedics products
* Perform CAD design and FEM simulations to analyze and optimize the components
* Oversee the overall picture of the entire device and coordinates the activities of internal and external CAD designers
* Design components and systems for optimal product design and manufacturability using advanced CAD tools
* Analyze the key parameters impacting the performances of the devices and the manufacturing properties in general
* Develop and validate computational models for organ perfusion systems
* Collaborate with other engineers to incorporate CAD and FEM analysis results into product designs
* Support verification testing and correlate with simulation results
* Document simulation methodologies and results for design history files
* Develop design recommendations based on CAD\-FEM analysis outcomes
* Design the components and the final products with a keen eye on the details impacting the critical characteristics of the device and the manufacturability
* Perform other TransMedics tasks and duties as assigned/required.

MANAGEMENT RESPONSIBILITIES

* This position will not have management responsibilities.


PHYSICAL ATTRIBUTES

* Standard engineering working environment.

MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

* Master's degree or PhD in Mechanical Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, or related field or high school diploma with extensive experience in the same role
* 15\+ years experience with CAD and FEM simulation for medical devices or similar applications
* Strong CAD modeling skills (SolidWorks is preferred)
* Knowledge of subsystems used in medical devices
* Experience validating CAD models with experimental data
* Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Good communication skills in English; Italian language skills preferred
* Ability to work in an international environment
* Highly motivated to enhance the products, the techniques and consequently the life of the patients
* Good knowledge of Microsoft Office suite;
* Willingness to relocate to Mirandola, Italy

***Every Organ Wasted is a Life Not Saved.***


TransMedics, Inc. is a commercial\-stage medical technology company transforming organ transplant therapy for patients worldwide. Our mission is to help save more patients’ lives by increasing access to viable donor organs for those who are awaiting an organ transplant. To accomplish this mission, we partner closely with transplant stakeholders worldwide and help expand their access to healthy donor organs, while delivering the highest quality technology, service, clinical care and outcomes.


Driven by a passion for improving patient care, we make the impossible possible and keep our employees at the center of everything we do. Together, we strive to enhance the quality of life for transplant recipients and their families, fostering hope and healing in the journey toward better health.

**Maximize your potential at TransMedics, Inc.**


www.TransMedics.com


TransMedics is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, marital status, age, disability or protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.
company_description: TransMedics is a publicly traded, medical technology company revolutionizing what’s possible in organ transplant through innovation, collaboration, and commitment.

title: Field Service Engineer
description:
This is a pivotal, hands\-on role at the intersection of production, R\&D, and customer support. You will be one of the first dedicated Field Service Engineers at Cellply, with the opportunity to help shape how we deliver, install, and maintain complex analytical instruments in the field.


In the near term, the role combines in\-house and on\-site work: you will test and qualify instruments before they ship, support production in resolving technical issues, and perform maintenance both in\-house and at customer sites. As our installed base grows, the role will become increasingly field\-focused, with installation, maintenance, and repair at customer sites becoming the core of the job, with travel evolving from below 50% today to above 50% over time. You will operate primarily across Europe and the Middle East, with possible expansion to North America and APAC as our footprint grows.


You will be a key link between customers, production, and the engineering team, ensuring system uptime and building trusted relationships with the scientists who rely on our technology.

 **Responsibilities:**

***In\-house***

* Test, verify, and qualify instruments before installation, ensuring they meet quality and performance standards.
* Support the production team in diagnosing and resolving technical issues on manufactured systems.
* Perform calibration and maintenance of instruments in\-house.
* Contribute to the improvement of the procedures and tools used during testing, calibration, and installation.
* Collaborate with R\&D and engineering to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues.
* Interface with external suppliers to manage technical and component issues.
* Capture customer insights and identify opportunities for product and process improvement.

***In the field / customer\-facing***

* Perform on\-site installations, preventive and corrective maintenance activities, repairs, and upgrades of VivaCyte systems at customer sites.
* Provide responsive, high\-quality technical support to customers, both remotely and on\-site.
* Manage logistics for systems, spare parts, and tools to ensure timely service delivery.
* Plan, document, and track all service activities.

  



  

  

**Requirements**  

* Technical diploma or degree in Mechanical Engineering, Automation/Mechatronics Engineering, Biomedical Engineering, or an equivalent technical discipline.
* 3–5 years of relevant experience, ideally in field service or in the manufacture and testing of complex analytical, life\-science, or high\-tech instrumentation.
* Solid, hands\-on, multidisciplinary technical ability across mechanical and electrical systems; experience with optical/imaging units or microfluidics is a strong plus.
* Good written and spoken English; additional languages are a plus.
* Willingness to travel (currently below 50%, expected to grow above 50%).
* A practical attitude for prototyping, mechanical assembly, and test execution.
* Strong problem\-solving and root\-cause analysis skills.
* Excellent interpersonal and communication skills with a customer\-first mindset.
* Self\-starter, organized, flexible, and comfortable in a fast\-paced start\-up environment.
* Experience with service ticketing or service\-management systems is a plus.

  

**Benefits**  

* Health Insurance
* Training and development on the field and through internal know\-how transfer, and the chance to grow with the company as our service organization scales internationally
* Exposure to breakthrough innovation in advanced therapies
